text: Continuous integration, or CI, is a software development practice where team members regularly merge their code changes into a central repository. A key part of CI is automated builds. Every time new code is pushed, a CI server automatically compiles the project and runs tests. This process is called a CI pipeline. By running tests automatically, developers can catch issues early, often within minutes of a change. This saves time compared to finding problems much later. CI helps teams maintain a more stable codebase and avoid the stress of trying to fix many bugs before a release.
